summary: Install.sh needs --version/-V support to print the installer version and exit 0 before any dependency checks or file writes. The feature follows the existing --help short-circuit pattern and requires only localized additions in argument parsing and usage text.
evidence: install.sh --help exits 0 before any git/gh calls (existing short-circuit pattern at lines 312-314)
bash .agent/scripts/validate.sh → exit 0, all engine tests pass
No VERSION file or version constant exists anywhere in the repository
Script argument parsing loop (lines 270-320) uses a case statement; unknown options hit the *) fail handler
ENGINE_REF default is v1, providing a natural version anchor for the installer
root_cause_or_rationale: The installer lacks a version inspection entry point, so users cannot verify which installer version they have without triggering the full install flow. Adding --version/-V gives users a zero-side-effect way to identify the installer before running it.
implementation_plan: Define a VERSION constant near the top of install.sh (after the other default variable declarations, around line 13): VERSION='v1'

Add a --version / -V case in the argument parsing loop (lines 312-314, immediately before or after the -h/--help case) that prints the version string and exits 0: printf 'install.sh version %s\n' "$VERSION" ; exit 0
Add a usage line in the usage() function (around line 33) documenting the new option: -V, --version Show installer version and exit
validation_plan: bash install.sh --version → prints version string and exit 0
bash install.sh -V → prints version string and exit 0
Verify --version does not create/modify/delete any files: git status --porcelain → empty
Verify --version does not require git repository context (run outside a repo if possible): fails only on unknown option before, not on missing git repo
Verify -h/--help still works and shows the new --version/-V line
Verify --engine-ref custom --version still prints the installer version (not the engine ref) and exits 0
shellcheck install.sh → exit 0 with no warnings
bash .agent/scripts/validate.sh → exit 0 (installs listener, runs YAML parse, all tests pass)
risks: The issue does not specify the exact version string to print. 'v1' is proposed as it matches the engine's default ENGINE_REF and is the most natural choice, but maintainer may prefer a different scheme such as a numeric version or git-derived tag.
